name: Slack
description: Integrates with Slack Web API for team messaging, channel management, and notification workflows
setup_instructions:
## Get a Slack bot token

Slack uses a **bot user OAuth token** (starts with `xoxb-`). You create a Slack app in your workspace, give it permission scopes, install it, then copy the token.

### 1. Create the Slack app
1. Open https://api.slack.com/apps and click **Create New App** → **From scratch**.
2. Name it (e.g. `T1 Agentics`) and pick the workspace it should live in. Click **Create App**.

### 2. Add bot scopes
1. In the left sidebar, click **OAuth & Permissions**.
2. Scroll to **Scopes → Bot Token Scopes** and click **Add an OAuth Scope** for each of:
   - `chat:write` — required to send messages
   - `chat:write.public` — post in channels the bot isn't a member of (optional but recommended)
   - `channels:read` — list public channels
   - `groups:read` — list private channels the bot is in
   - `users:read` — look up users by ID
   - `files:write` — only if you'll upload files

### 3. Install to workspace
1. Scroll back to the top of **OAuth & Permissions** and click **Install to Workspace**.
2. Approve the scopes. Slack returns you to the OAuth page showing a **Bot User OAuth Token** that starts with `xoxb-`.
3. Copy that token.

### 4. Invite the bot to channels
In Slack, open each channel you want the bot to post in and run:
```
/invite @T1 Agentics
```
(or whatever you named the app). `chat:write.public` lets it post in public channels without this, but private channels always require an invite.

### 5. Find channel IDs
T1 actions take channel IDs (e.g. `C01ABCDEF`), not `#channel-name`. To get one: right-click the channel in Slack → **View channel details** → scroll to the bottom — the ID is shown.

### 6. Paste the token below
Paste the **full `xoxb-...` token** in the **Token** field. Slack does not require a prefix — T1 sends it as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` automatically.

Click **Test Authentication** to confirm.

### Troubleshooting
- **`invalid_auth`**: Token is wrong, revoked, or the app was uninstalled. Re-install from **OAuth & Permissions**.
- **`not_in_channel`**: Bot can't post in that channel. `/invite` it, or add the `chat:write.public` scope and re-install.
- **`missing_scope`**: You added a scope after install. Re-install the app so the new scope takes effect.
- **`channel_not_found`**: You're passing a channel name instead of an ID. Use the `C…` ID from channel details.

actions.name: Send Message
actions.description: Send a message to a Slack channel or direct message

parameters.name: channel
parameters.description: Channel ID, DM ID, or conversation ID to post to

parameters.name: blocks
parameters.description: JSON array of Block Kit blocks for rich message formatting

parameters.name: thread_ts
parameters.description: Timestamp of parent message to reply in thread

actions.name: List Channels
actions.description: List all channels in the workspace accessible to the bot

parameters.name: types
parameters.description: Comma-separated channel types (public_channel, private_channel, mpim, im)

actions.name: Get Message
actions.description: Retrieve a specific message and its replies from a channel

parameters.name: ts
parameters.description: Timestamp of the message to retrieve

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Channel name (lowercase, no spaces, max 80 chars)

parameters.name: team_id
parameters.description: Team ID for Enterprise Grid workspaces

actions.name: Get Channel Info
actions.description: Get detailed information about a specific channel

actions.name: Update Message
actions.description: Update an existing message in a channel

parameters.name: ts
parameters.description: Timestamp of the message to update
